---
title: "Capital Without Category: Governance, Inequality, and the Micro-Enterprise Paradox in Brazil"
author:
  - name: Zhijun He
  - name: Jerry Miao
  - name: Paxton Boyd
date: 03/25/202
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: false
    theme: cosmo
    highlight-style: github
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    df-print: paged
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Project Description

Brazil's official firm-size classification (Simples Nacional)(https://thebrazilbusiness.com/article/taxation-regime-simples-nacional) sorts registered companies into micro, small, and medium/large tiers based on annual gross revenue. But revenue may be a poor proxy for capital endowment. A sole proprietor earning 360,000 BRL per year is legally a "micro-enterprise" regardless of whether they hold 10,000 BRL or 10,000,000 BRL in declared capital stock. This raises what might be called a classification paradox: the same regulatory label could encompass both subsistence operations and well-capitalized investment vehicles.

This project uses Brazil's CNPJ business registry(Cadastro Nacional da Pessoa Jurídica--National Registry of Legal Entities) (https://dados.gov.br/dados/conjuntos-dados/cadastro-nacional-da-pessoa-juridica---cnpj) — a near-census of all registered firms — to expose the internal heterogeneity that aggregate size categories conceal. We focus on two orthogonal dimensions that revenue-based classification ignores: **within-tier capital inequality** and **governance structure** (the legal role of the controlling owner). Our central question is whether "micro-enterprise" functions as a coherent economic category or a regulatory label that shelters firms with vastly different capital endowments under the same umbrella — with direct implications for how development policy should target capital support.

---

## Research Questions

1. **Within-tier inequality:** To what extent do capital endowments vary among micro-enterprises within the same regulatory tier? How does within-tier capital inequality, measured by the Gini coefficient, compare across firm-size classifications?

2. **Governance as a capital predictor:** How does owner qualification — Managing Partner, Beneficial Owner, or Sole Entrepreneur — relate to capital accumulation independently of firm size? To what extent does a governance premium persist within the same regulatory tier?

3. **The "capital-rich micro":** What combinations of legal form and governance archetype characterize micro-enterprises with unusually high declared capital stock? What does this pattern suggest about how development programs might better identify genuinely capital-constrained firms?

---

## Dataset

**Brazilian Companies (CNPJ)**

The dataset is drawn from Brazil's *Cadastro Nacional da Pessoa Jurídica* (CNPJ), the federal business registry administered by the Receita Federal (Federal Revenue Service). It contains registration records for firms across Brazil with the following key variables:

The dataset is publicly available via the [TidyTuesday GitHub repository](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday) and requires no restricted access or IRB approval
| Variable | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `capital_social` | Numeric (BRL) | Declared capital stock at registration |
| `porte_empresa` | Categorical | Firm size tier under Simples Nacional (Micro, Small, Medium/Large) |
| `natureza_juridica` | Categorical | Legal form (LLC, sole proprietorship, EIRELI, etc.) |
| `owner_qualification` | Categorical | Legal role of controlling owner (Managing Partner, Beneficial Owner, Sole Entrepreneur, Other) |
| `municipio` | Categorical | Municipality of registration |
| `cnae_fiscal` | Categorical | Primary sector code (CNAE classification) |

The working subset used in this project contains **141,332 observations** after standard cleaning (removal of records with missing or zero capital stock, harmonization of legal-form codes). Capital stock is right-skewed and will be log-transformed for distributional analysis. The `owner_qualification` field is recoded into four governance archetypes: Managing Partner / Administrator, Sole Entrepreneur, Beneficial Owner (passive/investor), and Other.

---

## Inspiration

This project grew out of a prior TidyTuesday analysis that identified a macro-level capital paradox: micro-enterprises constitute the majority of registered Brazilian firms yet control a disproportionately small share of total capital stock. That finding raises a harder question — is this gap primarily *between* size tiers, *within* them, or both simultaneously?

The answer matters for development policy. If the capital deficit is concentrated among a genuine subset of micro-enterprises while others are actually well-capitalized holding-company subsidiaries registered as "micro" because their revenue falls below the Simples Nacional threshold, then blanket micro-enterprise subsidies are a blunt and potentially misdirected instrument. This project is motivated by the broader development economics question of *when categorical targeting works and when it fails* — a question with relevance well beyond Brazil, extending to informal economy support programs across Sub-Saharan Africa, South Asia, and Latin America.

---

## Communication Plan

- **Primary channel:** Google group chat (backup: email)
- **Expected response time:** Within 24 hours on weekdays; best-effort on weekends
- **Standing meeting:** Wednesdays, 7:00–8:00 PM (location: TBD or Zoom)
- **Async coordination:** GitHub Issues and pull request comments for all code-related decisions; brief meeting notes shared in group chat after each session
- **Milestone check-ins:** 48-hour heads-up in group chat before any submission deadline

---

## Roles

All team members are expected to contribute substantively to analysis, writing, and visualization. Leads exist for accountability, not sole ownership.

| Role | Person | Responsibilities |
|---|---|---|
| **Point of Contact** | Zhijun He | Primary email contact with instructor and preceptors; represents team in official communications; coordinates submission logistics |
| **Technical Lead** | Jerry Miao | Oversees GitHub repository structure; reviews and merges pull requests; maintains the Quarto website; ensures full reproducibility of the pipeline |
| **Style Lead** | Paxton Boyd | Enforces the COMP/STAT 112/212 Code Styling Guide across all `.qmd` and `.R` files; reviews all code before submission milestones |

---

## Conflict Resolution

**Missed internal deadlines:** If a team member anticipates missing an internal deadline, they notify the group chat at least 24 hours in advance with a revised timeline. The team redistributes the task for the current milestone; the member makes up the contribution before the next deliverable. Repeated missed deadlines — defined as two or more without prior notice — are escalated to the instructor following a team conversation.

**Disagreements (50/50 split):** Each side presents their case for up to five minutes, then the team votes again. If still tied:

- **Code and methodology questions** → Technical Lead (Jerry Miao) has the deciding vote.
- **Framing, writing, and communication questions** → Point of Contact (Zhijun He) has the deciding vote.

If the disagreement is substantive enough that neither tie-breaker feels appropriate, we consult the instructor or a preceptor before the next deadline.

**General norm:** Raise concerns early and in the group chat — not in GitHub comments or after a milestone has been submitted.

---

## Implementation Plan

The following tasks are to be posted as individual GitHub Issues in the project repository, tagged with their corresponding milestone label and assignee(s). Dates assume a Week 1 start of **February 24, 2026**.

| Issue | Task | Due Date | Lead |
|---|---|---|---|
| #1 | Initialize GitHub repo; set up Quarto site skeleton; add `proposal.qmd` template | Mar 1 | Jerry (Technical) |
| #2 | Draft and submit Milestone 1 proposal; post GitHub Issues #3–#12 | Mar 7 | All (Zhijun submits) |
| #3 | Data ingestion and cleaning: filter, recode `owner_qualification`, log-transform capital | Mar 12 | Jerry (Technical) |
| #4 | Exploratory data analysis: distributions, missingness audit, outlier assessment | Mar 14 | All |
| #5 | Build Visualization 1: Lorenz curves by firm size with Gini annotations | Mar 18 | Zhijun |
| #6 | Build Visualization 2: Governance premium by size tier (boxplots or violin plots) | Mar 18 | Jerry |
| #7 | Build Visualization 3: Capital-rich micro profile by legal form × governance | Mar 20 | Paxton |
| #8 | Milestone 2: EDA write-up, narrative captions, and site integration | Mar 22 | All (Paxton style review) |
| #9 | Regression / modeling: estimate governance premium controlling for size and sector | Apr 2 | Jerry |
| #10 | Capital-rich micro analysis: logistic model or conditional means by archetype | Apr 4 | Zhijun |
| #11 | Milestone 3: Analysis write-up draft; incorporate modeling results into narrative | Apr 10 | All (Paxton style review) |
| #12 | Peer review incorporation and targeted revision | Apr 18 | Zhijun coordinates |
| #13 | Final website polish: styling, accessibility, consistent theme | Apr 24 | Paxton + Jerry |
| #14 | Milestone 4: Final submission; verify reproducibility end-to-end | Apr 27 | All (Zhijun submits) |
| #15 | Presentation prep: slides or recorded walkthrough | Apr 27 | All |

> **Note:** GitHub Issues should be created in the project repository with appropriate labels (`milestone-1`, `milestone-2`, etc.) and assignees matching the Lead column above. The Technical Lead (Jerry) is responsible for setting up the issue tracker by the **April 1** deadline.
